response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack/web engineer with 15+ years of experience, a CS bachelor's degree, and some systems-level exposure (low-latency DLang services, C/C++ at National Instruments, embedded mentions). However, the role requires SoC and IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, and deep-learning workload performance analysis for AI accelerators — none of which appear in his background. His performance work is at the web-services level (async I/O, caching), not silicon or accelerator architecture, making this a fundamental domain mismatch for a principal-level hardware performance role.
strengths: Extensive engineering experience (15+ years) meeting the BS + 8 years experience bar
Low-latency systems work handling millions of HTTP requests/sec with async I/O and caching
Some C/C++ and embedded/hardware-adjacent experience (LabWindows/CVI, robotic arms)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep neural network training/inference or AI accelerator workload background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Solid systems and low-latency engineering fundamentals
